results.name: Simulate and Test CDS User Needs & Scenarios
results.description_html:
CDS User Scenarios Simulation and Testing Summary

Simulated 3 realistic user scenarios from the product requirements in docs/prd/ and tested querying the PostgreSQL CDS database to retrieve target fields, validating the commercial and educational value of the CDS data asset.

Key Accomplishments:

  • Scenario 1: Elite STEM Competitiveness (Stanford vs. MIT vs. Caltech): Validated that Caltech test-blind policy is handled gracefully as an explicit NULL (triggering UI score CONSIDERATION warnings), while MIT and Stanford show top-75th percentile profiles.
  • Scenario 2: Need-Based cost calculation (Princeton vs. Harvard vs. Duke): Computed true net costs showing Princeton ($13,129 out-of-pocket) and Harvard ($11,822) packages. Gracefully raised a Data Gap warning for Duke which declared costs_available=false in Section G.
  • Scenario 3: Transfer Credit and Selectivity (UPenn vs. Columbia): Demonstrated Columbia's transfer acceptance rate (~8.97%) is 2.8x higher than UPenn's (~3.21%), and retrieved exact credit transfer grades (C for Columbia, 2.0 for UPenn) and credit limitations.

New/Modified Files:

  • scratch/test_user_scenarios.py (NEW - testing query script)
  • verify_cds_user_scenarios.md (NEW - persistent markdown audit verification report in artifacts)

results.name: PRD-112: Common Data Set Data Asset Integration
results.description_html:
PRD-112: Common Data Set Requirements & Implementation

This record tracks the product requirements and technical implementation of the Common Data Set (CDS) data asset inside CollegeFlow, fully aligned with the requirements in PRD-112-common-data-set-cds.md.

Asset Specification & Product Boundaries:

  • Source Provenance: University-published official Common Data Set reports (2024-2025).
  • Value Proposition: Powers admissions selectivity, class profiles, financial aid, and transfer admissions contexts.
  • Zero Data Loss Condition: All elements (objects, lists, empty arrays, null disclosures) recursively parsed and mapped cleanly with 0 data loss.

Technical Deliverables & Verification:

  • DDL Setup: PostgreSQL-native database schemas with dynamic section seeding from init_pg_db.py.
  • Ingestion Engine: Clean dot-path recursion, dynamic dictionary expansion, and reverse mapping resolution in import_to_pg.py.
  • Audit & Verification: Clean CLI reports verifying stored_value_count = canonical_leaf_count and missing_cnt = 0 across all 16 target universities (totaling 10,119 facts).

results.name: CDS PostgreSQL Schema Ingestion and Verification Implementation
results.description_html:
CDS PostgreSQL Ingestion and Verification Summary

Successfully implemented the 100% zero-data-loss flat fact schema for the Common Data Set (CDS) in PostgreSQL, fully conforming to the specs in cds_schema_design.md and cds_schema.sql.

Key Accomplishments:

  • PostgreSQL DB Setup: Created database/init_pg_db.py and dynamically discovered section codes from the canonical schema key layout, dynamically seeding metadata definitions cleanly.
  • Robust Ingestion Engine: Built database/import_to_pg.py leveraging recursive deep traversal of JSON trees to flatten all structures (including objects, lists, empty structures, and explicit nulls) into cds_values facts. Reverse mapped all key configurations.
  • Audit & Verification: Authored database/verify_pg_integrity.py, ensuring that stored_value_count = canonical_leaf_count, missing_value_count = 0, and doc SHA256 checksums matched perfectly.

Audit Execution Output:

Total target institutions verified: 16
Total stored facts (values): 10,119
INTEGRITY AUDIT PASSED: Zero data loss verified across all loaded CDS documents.

Modified/New Files:

  • database/cds_schema_design.md (optimized)
  • database/cds_schema.sql (optimized)
  • database/init_pg_db.py (NEW)
  • database/import_to_pg.py (NEW)
  • database/verify_pg_integrity.py (NEW)

  • Implemented secure token hash/encryption requirements: Session tokens hashed (tokenHash), OTP challenges hashed (codeHash), and Account social tokens encrypted using AES-256-GCM.
  • Fully converted bare Strings into Prisma Enums (UserRole, UserType, Region, SubscriptionStatus, BoardMemberRole, BoardMemberStatus, PaymentProvider, SubscriptionAction, SubscriptionEventStatus, AuditAction, ActorType).
  • Tightened Sponsor penetration boundaries to require COUNSELOR_PRO and Accepted Board status.
  • Introduced BoardMember relationship mapping to elegant support parent, student, and advisor three-party collaborations, overcoming the 1:1 limit.
  • Designed an order-level idempotency key for SubscriptionService and formalized endsAt math calculation.
  • Added comprehensive security testing strategies, referral anti-abuse policies, entitlement resolution, authorization matrices, and retention/PII policies.

Verification Results

results.name: Fix registration failure on create account
results.description_html:
Registration failure diagnosis

User reported the create account page showing generic registration failure.

Findings

  • At the start of diagnosis, only Vite was running on port 38030; the BFF on port 38090 was not running. The registration page posts to /api/auth/sign-up/email, which Vite proxies to the BFF.
  • After starting the BFF with BFF_PORT=38090 npm run dev:bff, direct BFF registration and Vite-proxied registration both returned 200 with disposable test emails.
  • The user email ggdayup@gmail.com was checked read-only and was not present in either Better Auth user or app User tables at diagnosis time.

Verification

  • POST http://127.0.0.1:38090/api/auth/sign-up/email with disposable test email: 200.
  • POST http://127.0.0.1:38030/api/auth/sign-up/email through Vite proxy: 200.
  • Headless Chrome registration page submission with disposable test email: 200 response and page navigation to home.
  • Current BFF health check GET /api/auth/me: 401 as expected for anonymous, confirming BFF is responding.
  • Current Vite register page /register: 200.
  • Disposable registration test users were deleted from Better Auth and app user tables.

No source change was required for this report; the immediate cause was the BFF process not running after the previous smoke-test cleanup.

results.name: Full frontend/backend smoke test after IPEDS audit fixes
results.description_html:
Completed full frontend/backend smoke test

Ran API-level and browser-level smoke tests after the IPEDS audit fixes. The first browser run exposed two real blockers, both fixed and retested successfully.

Issues found and fixed

  • BFF failed to start because requireAdmin was declared twice in server/server.ts. Removed the duplicate and kept the stricter disabled-account-aware middleware.
  • Better Auth registration failed with relation "user" does not exist. Added Better Auth core table migration for user, session, account, and verification.
  • Better Auth proxied login from Vite returned 403 due invalid origin http://127.0.0.1:38030. Added trusted frontend origins to Better Auth config.
  • Guest homepage crashed on null user.emailVerified. Changed the verification guard to use optional access.
  • Admin password reset query targeted "user"."password", but Better Auth stores credential passwords in "account". Corrected the query.

API smoke results

  • GET /api/universities: 200 and public payload has no cipCode, cipTitle, sourceType, sourceSystem, or verificationId.
  • Anonymous GET /api/admin/ipeds/release-status: 401.
  • Logged-in FREE user GET /api/admin/ipeds/release-status: 403.
  • Logged-in ADMIN user GET /api/admin/ipeds/release-status: 200.
  • Logged-in ADMIN user GET /api/admin/ipeds/candidates?limit=3: 200.

Browser smoke results

  • Guest homepage renders and does not show IPEDS Admin.
  • Non-admin smoke user can log in and does not see IPEDS Admin.
  • Admin smoke user can log in, sees IPEDS Admin, opens IPEDS Admin Audit, and the UI fetches /api/admin/ipeds/release-status with 200.
  • Public UI text check found no technical source leakage: no visible CIP, IPEDS UNITID, IPEDS Bachelor, IPEDS-derived, Wikidata Entity, Verified ID, or generated AUDIT IDs.

Verification

  • npm run lint passed.
  • npx prisma migrate deploy passed.
  • npx prisma migrate status reports schema up to date.
  • npm run build passed with only the existing chunk-size warning.
  • Targeted git diff --check passed.
  • Temporary smoke users were deleted from both Better Auth and app user tables.

Artifacts

results.name: 邀请码体验
推荐用户的奖励
用户付费、订阅
用户的注册、登录、信息收集
results.description_html:
Problem Statement

升学顾问在为学生交付专业、选校及 ROI 决策方案时，通常散落在 Excel、Notion 和微信聊天中，缺乏标准化、可视化且可传播的交付工具，导致工作效率低下，且家长对决策依据的信任度不足。同时，免费学生用户转化 PRO 订阅的生命周期价值有限，而高频交付的顾问才是最核心的付费群体与增长杠杆。

Solution

将平台定位从单纯的“用户管理与登录”升级为以“顾问交付系统”为轴心的 B2B2C 选校决策 SaaS。引入 Decision Engine + CRM Workspace + Sponsored Access + Milestone 6-Page Print Report。让顾问可以在 30 分钟内完成一个学生的第一份阶段性选校报告，并让免费学生在看板内享受顾问赞助的数据穿透，触发个人 PRO 转化与裂变传播。

Key User Stories

  • P0 基础层：支持手机号 OTP（国内）与 Google 一键登录（国际）手动切换；保存成绩、RIASEC 代码及财务预算；生成完备度与可信度评分，保留 Decision Timeline 决策轨迹。
  • P1a 核心 CRM 闭环：三角色 Onboarding 最短路径分流；任务驱动型今日工作台带一键 Action Buttons；4 阶段 Admission Workspace（背景、策略、评审、交付）带严格解锁门槛；强制性的 6 级 Counselor Rationale 校验；极简家长决策卡（Approve/Discuss/Question Asked/Reject）；网页端 6 页交付报告带 Report Readiness Score 与轻量顾问个人品牌。
  • P1b 效率增强：14 天有效期无 PII 加密分享链接；Advisor 深度品牌个性化（Logo、Tagline）；Action Checklist 任务与具体申请强绑定；Boilerplate 推荐语模版库；Workspace Archive 归档与 quota 自动释还。
  • P2 后置规划：Teacher 角色高中 Prerequisite 自动比对；GeoIP 自动路由；WeChat/Apple OAuth 登录；ledger 式 before/after 变动 diff 记录。

Implementation Decisions

  • 采用 Better Auth 原生 Cookie 会话管理，密码单向哈希，第三方 token AES-256 加密。
  • 手动 China / Global 切换，国内 OTP Redis 防刷，校验失败自动锁定。
  • 引入解耦式多角色看板，通过 BoardCollaboration 穿透受邀 Workspace 内的高级数据，外部实施 global paywall。
  • 极简 Parent View，不提供独立 dashboard，强制拦截为 Cost+ROI+Risk+Counselor Rec 确认视图。
  • 计费集成 Stripe & 国内扫码，新增 User quota 字段及 isArchived 归档状态。

Testing Decisions

  • 使用 supertest 对 Express 端点进行真实 HTTP 模拟请求及 DB 状态校验。
  • 校验密码重置后全部 Session 强退失效。
  • 校验 OTP 5 次校验失败锁定机制。
  • 校验免费学生在 sponsored workspace 内外的高级数据拦截/穿透边界。

Out of Scope

多租户学区多校统一管理；Teacher 角色及教学工作流；前端自研 PDF 渲染（交由 window.print()）；multiplayer 即时游标；WeChat/Apple OAuth 登录。

results.name: Evaluate IPEDS 2024-25 Provisional database integration strategy
results.description_html:
Task Completed: IPEDS 2024-25 Integration Evaluation

Evaluated how to integrate the NCES/IPEDS 2024-25 Provisional Access database into the system through a source-of-truth grilling session and recorded the accepted architecture in ADR-005.

Modified Files

  • docs/adr/ADR-005-ipeds-raw-mirror-and-product-projection.md - Added the accepted IPEDS raw mirror, curated projection, metrics, provenance, program-field, eligibility, ETL, validation, and implementation roadmap decision.

  • docs/adr/README.md - Added ADR-005 to the ADR index and added a compliance gate preventing IPEDS-derived product fields without the ADR-005 safeguards.

Key Decisions

  • IPEDS will be preserved as a full raw mirror with metadata and curated projections, using ipeds_raw, ipeds_meta, and ipeds_curated schemas.

  • Raw/meta IPEDS tables stay out of Prisma; Prisma manages product-domain tables such as structured metrics, metric definitions, identifiers, CIP mappings, candidates, and publication decisions.

  • IPEDS UNITID anchors U.S. institutions externally but does not replace internal University.id.

  • IPEDS-derived program fields may display on the front end using natural names, while admin/audit layers retain CIP, source type, release, table, variable, and provenance details.

  • Official catalog crawling becomes supplemental for exact official names, school divisions, course requirements, and newly launched or renamed programs.

  • Old wide metrics such as averageCost, gradRate, and medianSalary are deprecated as product read/write targets in favor of registered structured metrics with value status and missing-reason semantics.

  • Provisional and final IPEDS releases coexist; final data switches active projections rather than overwriting provisional data.

Verification Results

  • Read the supplied IPEDS README and tables workbook metadata to confirm release type, table documentation, variable metadata, and value-set coverage.

  • Inspected the current Prisma schema, Scorecard ingest, BFF mapping, and ADRs to align the decision with existing architecture.

results.description_html:
✅ Roadmap Audit Completed & Optimization Plan Formulated

We have completed a comprehensive technical audit of the remaining Phase 1, 2, and 3 tasks in the Collage Major project roadmap. Below is a summary of the critical bottlenecks identified and the high-performance optimizations formulated:

🔍 Critical Bottlenecks & Solutions

  • Issue 11: DB Schema Mapping: University majors do not map 1:1 to national standard CIP/MoE codes. Solution: Formulated a robust UniversityMajorAssociation junction table schema with mapping similarity score tracking and validation flags.

results.name: [Phase 2] Implement Hybrid Lexical & Vector Mapping (BM25 + Cosine Similarity)
results.description_html:
✅ Phase 2 Task Completed: Implement Hybrid Lexical & Vector Mapping (BM25 + Cosine Similarity)

We have successfully designed, implemented, and verified the high-performance Hybrid Matcher combining BM25 Lexical Keyword search and pgvector Vector Cosine Similarity.

📂 Modified Files

  • backend/requirements.txt: Appended necessary machine learning and NLP libraries: numpy, sentence-transformers, rank-bm25, and pgvector.
  • backend/matcher.py: Implemented the core HybridMatcher engine combining BM25 keyword matching with pgvector-based database embeddings queries. Added database initialization, embedding seeding helpers, and auto-coupling methods.
  • backend/test_coupling.py: Built a robust integration test suite simulating real-world custom major ingestion, automatic mapping, and scoring.

💡 Key Decisions & Architecture

  • Dual-Engine Architecture: Lexical matching (BM25) ensures precise matching on direct keyword alignments and acronyms/abbreviations, while semantic matching (pgvector cosine similarity using sentence-transformers/paraphrase-multilingual-MiniLM-L12-v2) guarantees conceptual alignment across languages (bilingual English/Chinese).
  • Optimized Combining Strategy: Lexical and semantic scores are normalized and combined using configurable weights (default: 40% lexical weight, 60% semantic weight). Negative similarities are gracefully handled.
  • Robust Connection Processing: Cleans direct connection strings from Prisma parameters (like ?schema=public) to prevent native PostgreSQL driver initialization errors, and executes initial setups with vector support dynamically.

🧪 Verification & Integration Results

  • E2E integration tests ran flawlessly. All 5 mock custom majors were successfully mapped to correct standard national majors with high confidence scores:
    • Advanced Computational Data Science & Machine Learning → Computer science (Score: 0.7118, Audited: False)
    • Honors Electrical & Computer Systems Engineering → Electrical engineering (Score: 0.8509, Audited: False)
    • Bilingual Business Communication & Global Media Studies → Communications and mass media (Score: 0.7773, Audited: False)
    • 应用数学与计算科学 (Applied Math & Computing) → Applied mathematics (Score: 0.8929, Audited: False)
    • 临床医学与公共卫生管理 (Clinical Medicine & Health Administration) → Community and public health (Score: 0.8062, Audited: False)
  • Validation checks confirmed that the newly coupled records are correctly kept with isValidated = False to support secondary manual review/audits.

results.name: [Phase 1] Integrate U.S. College Scorecard & IPEDS API with Off-Serverless Runner
results.description_html:
✅ Task Completed: U.S. College Scorecard & IPEDS API Sync Ingestion Runner

We have successfully implemented and verified the standalone background ingestion runner that connects to the official U.S. Department of Education College Scorecard REST API and synchronizes outcomes directly with PostgreSQL.

📁 Modified Files:

  • src/ingest/collegeScorecardIngest.ts (Created: Ingestion Runner script)

🛠️ Key Decisions & Architectural Highlights:

  • Standalone & Off-Serverless: Built as a modular TypeScript script executable via npx tsx src/ingest/collegeScorecardIngest.ts, separating API querying from live request-response web threads.

  • Prisma v7 Driver Adapter Integration: Integrated standard PostgreSQL connection handling using @prisma/adapter-pg and connection pooling via pg, conforming strictly to the modern, performant "Rust-free" engine architecture of Prisma v7.

  • Batch Processing (Chunk size: 20): Dynamically groups IPEDS/Scorecard IDs into batches of 20 to hit the API, minimizing request count and staying safely within rate limits.

  • Dry-Run Mode: Added support for the --dry-run CLI flag to pull/display metrics for the first 5 schools in the console without committing database writes.

  • Robust Error Coercion: Gracefully checks API response codes (such as HTTP 429), ignores missing unit matches, and handles suppressed/non-numeric outcomes safely using strict type checking.

🔬 Verification Results:

  • Tested execution in dry-run mode: successfully retrieved and printed metrics for Columbia, Harvard, Princeton, Rice, and Stanford in beautiful, human-readable formatting.

  • Tested execution in production mode: synced all 9 matched universities, caching their Cost of Attendance, Graduation Rate, and Median Salary values directly in the database.

  • Verified PostgreSQL state: checked table properties to confirm all values were written with maximum numerical precision.

results.name: [Phase 1] Implement Cursor-Paginated Wikidata SPARQL Ingest Engine with Static Fallback
results.description_html:
✅ Task Completed: Wikidata SPARQL Ingest Engine

We have successfully designed, built, and verified the high-performance Wikidata SPARQL Ingestion Engine at src/ingest/wikidataIngest.ts. The engine connects securely and robustly to the public Wikidata endpoint and populates the database dynamically with zero errors.

📁 Modified/Added Files:

  • src/ingest/wikidataIngest.ts (Added) - Ingest engine script supporting cursor-based pagination, exponential retry backoff, and local fallback hydration.

  • prisma.config.ts (Updated) - Configured support for dynamic environment variable loading and dotenv for Prisma 7 compatibility.

  • prisma/schema.prisma (Updated) - Standardized provider and removed deprecated url parameters in accordance with Prisma 7 migration guidelines.

🛠️ Key Decisions & Architecture Highlights:

  • Cursor-Paginated Batches: Querying Wikidata in pages of 50 using SPARQL LIMIT and OFFSET parameters to bypass standard 60-second timeouts.

  • Exponential Retry Backoff: Implemented standard robust fetch wrapper that retries requests up to 3 times with progressive delay backing off on rate limits (429) or timeouts.

  • Double-check self-healing Upsert: Configured a safe database upsert mechanism using Prisma Client to avoid unique constraint violations on nameEn when Wikidata returns duplicate names for different entities.

  • Prisma 7 Compatibility: Leveraged Prisma 7's new centralized database configuration and Node-Postgres Driver Adapter (PrismaPg) for high-performance enterprise integration.

  • Static Fallback Hydration: If the external Wikidata endpoint is unreachable, the engine automatically hydrates the database using the local elite universities seed data from src/data/universitiesData.ts.

  • Dry-run Capability: Fully supports standard CLI argument --dry-run to print parsed records cleanly to console without applying db writes.

🧪 Verification Results:

  • Tested dry-run: npx tsx src/ingest/wikidataIngest.ts --dry-run successfully fetched and formatted 50 records in 37.73 seconds.

  • Tested production pipeline: npx tsx src/ingest/wikidataIngest.ts --pages=1 processed and successfully upserted 50 new university records into the PostgreSQL database with zero constraint issues.
